fact: The Ringtheatre was lit by open gas flames, not electric lights
source: Wien Geschichte Wiki, City of Vienna – Ringtheater

fact: The fire started when a stagehand's lamp ignited a backdrop/scenery
source: Wien Geschichte Wiki, City of Vienna – Ringtheaterbrand

fact: The fire climbed into the fly tower (Schnürboden) above the stage
The safety curtain (iron curtain / Eiserner Vorhang) jammed partway down and failed to seal the stage from the auditorium
At least 384 people died in the fire
source: Wien Geschichte Wiki, City of Vienna – Ringtheaterbrand (official estimates range from 384 to over 400)

fact: The safety curtain had never been tested before the disaster
source: Widely documented in accounts of the fire; discussed in Peter W. Jansen's and other German-language histories of Viennese theatre safety reform. Also noted in Wikipedia's summary of the disaster.
